revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:土師ニサンザイ古墳]][[zh:土師御陵古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Haze Nisanzai Kofun zenkei.JPG|location={{ill|Kita-ku|qid=Q1042504|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.546919|N|135.498547|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}}}
'''Haze Nisanzai Kofun''' (土師ニサンザイ古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped Kofun) located in {{ill|Kita-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市北区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-ku, Sakai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} and is managed as a {{ill|Ryo-bo sanko-chi|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryo-bo sanko-chi|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}} by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}.
The tumulus is also a designated National Historic Site and a part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site titled "Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group: Mounded Tombs of Ancient Japan."
== Overview ==
The Haze Nisanzai Kofun is situated at the southeastern edge of the Mozu Kofun Group and features a west-facing orientation. It is one of the largest tumuli in Japan, with an estimated original mound length exceeding 300 meters, placing it among the top seven largest kofun nationwide.<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）] (Sakai City Official Website, updated May 13, 2018).</ref>
The origins of the name "Nisanzai" remain unclear, but references to it appear from the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} onward. The term is thought to be a corruption of "misasagi" (陵), referring to imperial tombs.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000261206|title=古墳の名称の由来について知りたい。／レファレンス協同データーベース|accessdate=2021-11-19|publisher=National Diet Library (Sakai Municipal Library).}}</ref>
The mound has a three-tiered structure with a shield-shaped moat. The rear circular portion has a diameter of approximately 156 meters, and the front rectangular portion has a width of about 224 meters, making the front section notably larger than the rear. The current mound length is 290 meters, but archaeological surveys indicate it originally measured over 300 meters.<ref>[http://www.asahi.com/culture/update/1127/OSK201211270083.html "ニサンザイ古墳、300メートル超えか、国内7位に"] (Asahi Shimbun Digital).</ref>
The tumulus features {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (stone coverings) and an arrangement of cylindrical and figurative {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including depictions of houses, shields, and birds.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nisanzai.html|title=ニサンザイ古墳 【世界文化遺産 構成資産】|accessdate=2021-11-19|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Bureau.}}</ref>
== Cultural Significance ==
The Haze Nisanzai Kofun is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as the "Higashi Mozu Ryo-bo Sanko-chi" (東百舌鳥陵墓参考地), presumed to be a vacant tomb associated with {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|en|Emperor Hanzei|qid=Q329769|lt=Emperor Hanzei|1=Emperor Hanzei}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nisanzai.html|title=ニサンザイ古墳|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Bureau}}</ref> However, no clear archaeological evidence supports this designation.
== Features ==
=== Mound Dimensions === * '''Mound Length:''' Approximately 290 meters (restored length 300.3 meters). * '''Rear Circular Section Diameter:''' 156 meters. * '''Front Rectangular Section Width:''' 224 meters.
=== Moats === The mound is surrounded by a double moat, although only the inner moat is clearly visible today. Excavations have confirmed the presence of large wooden bridges across the moat, likely used for funeral processions.<ref>[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXNASDG2103X_R20C13A2CR8000/ "堺のニサンザイ古墳の周濠に柱穴列 5世紀、葬送用の橋か"] (Nikkei, February 21, 2013).</ref>
== Excavations and Restoration ==
From 2012 to 2015, a series of surveys and restorations were conducted to a11ress erosion caused by water waves in the surrounding moat. These efforts included protective revetments and landscaping to preserve the mound's original structure while maintaining its aesthetic integrity.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ|title=世界遺産／文化遺産オンライン|accessdate=2021-11-14|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Access ==
* '''Rail:''' 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Nakamozu Station|en|Nakamozu Station|qid=Q1863087|lt=Nakamozu Station|1=Nakamozu Station}} (Nankai Koya Line and Osaka Metro Midosuji Line). * '''Bus:''' From {{ill|Sakaihigashi Station|en|Sakaihigashi Station|qid=Q7402783|lt=Sakai-higashi Station|1=Sakaihigashi Station}} or {{ill|Fukai Station|en|Fukai Station|qid=Q5507572|lt=Fukai Station|1=Fukai Station}}, alight at Mozu-Umecho 3-chome.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
- {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} - {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} - {{ill|Higashi Mozu Ryo-bo Sanko-chi|ja|東百舌鳥陵墓参考地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Mozu Ryo-bo Sanko-chi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/nisanzai.html Official Site - Haze Nisanzai Kofun (Sakai City)] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ UNESCO World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group]
